patterns.name: Hesitation with Dense Word Problems
patterns.what: Jayron tends to leave questions blank when they involve longer text or multiple conditions, which can feel a bit overwhelming at first glance.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: Left the question blank instead of breaking down the steps for the paint mixture.
Skipped this fraction word problem, missing the chance to draw a simple model.
Did not attempt this pattern question about toys and sweets.

patterns.strategic_advice: When you see a long word problem, **read it one sentence at a time**. Use the **CUBES method** (Circle numbers, Underline the question, Box math action words). Even if you aren't sure of the final answer, **draw a simple model or write down the first calculation** to get started and earn partial marks.

patterns.name: Skipping Foundational Number Rules
patterns.what: Jayron occasionally skips straightforward questions about place values, rounding, and factors, missing out on easy marks.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: Left this place value question blank instead of writing out the digits.
Skipped this rounding question, which could have been solved by testing the options.
Did not attempt to find the common multiple, which just requires listing the times tables.

patterns.strategic_advice: When asked to write numerals or find place values, **draw a quick place value chart** (Ones, Tens, Hundreds, etc.) and fill it in. For factors and multiples, **list them out systematically** on the side of your paper. Don't leave these blank—your first instinct is often right!

patterns.name: Uncertainty with Geometric Diagrams
patterns.what: Jayron sometimes finds it tricky to interpret geometric figures, such as confusing slanted sides with perpendicular heights, or leaves spatial drawing tasks unattempted.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: Selected the slanted side AC instead of the perpendicular height AD.
Left the question blank instead of looking for the right-angle symbol to identify the base.
Did not attempt to draw the 2D views of the 3D stacked cubes.

patterns.strategic_advice: When looking for a height or base in a triangle, always hunt for the **right-angle square symbol**—heights must be straight up and down, never slanted! For 3D drawing tasks, **count the blocks layer by layer** and sketch lightly in pencil first.

patterns.name: Leaving Measurement Formulas Unapplied
patterns.what: Jayron frequently leaves volume and measurement questions blank, even when all the necessary dimensions are provided in the question.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: Skipped calculating the volume despite being given the height and base dimensions.
Left the volume calculation blank instead of applying the formula.
Did not attempt to find the missing water volume, missing a chance to multiply the given numbers.

patterns.strategic_advice: Whenever you see words like 'volume' or 'capacity', immediately write down the formula **Length × Width × Height**. Even if the question has extra steps, **multiply the given dimensions** to show your understanding and secure method marks.
